title: Understand: Reconstruction: The Fifteenth Amendment
description: Check your understanding of the Fifteenth Amendment with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: America’s Second Founding: Slavery, the Civil War, Emancipation, and Reconstruction

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which man proposed a draft of the Fifteenth Amendment that would have protected the right to vote on the grounds of race, color, nativity, property, education, and creed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Henry Wilson
Andrew Johnson
Abraham Lincoln
Andrew Jackson

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the name of the system of laws passed to suppress the Black vote after the ratification of the Fifteenth Amendment and a period of widespread political participation by African Americans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Black Codes
Secession Acts
Jim Crow
The Intolerable Acts

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the purpose of the Fifteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To end racial discrimination in voting
To limit the right to vote to men
To cancel the Elections Clause in Article I of the Constitution
To give Congress the power to enforce the end of slavery

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did some women’s rights activists, like Susan B. Anthony and Elizabeth Cady Stanton, oppose the Fifteenth Amendment?	**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It did not end sex discrimination in voting.
It gave Black women the right to vote.
It did not do enough to end financial barriers to voting.
It did not include a minimum age requirement.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true of the Reconstruction Acts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They applied to the former Confederate states.
They affected only voting rights.
They were not enforceable by Congress.
The Supreme Court ruled them unconstitutional.
A series of acts passed between 1867 and 1868 that set the requirements ex-Confederate states had to meet to rejoin the Union
A set of amendments that ended slavery, granted citizenship to Black people, and gave Black men the right to vote
A group of acts that ended the Civil War and required the southern states to make payments directly to formerly enslaved people
A collection of laws that detailed the conditions new territories must meet to be accepted as states

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the effect of the ratification of the Fifteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Black representation increased in the southern states.
The southern states rejoined the Union
Women received the right to vote.
The institution of slavery was outlawed in all states.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did the Reconstruction Acts require southern states to do?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Extend political rights to Black men
Ratify the Fourteenth Amendment
Submit to military rule
Agree to replace all of their representatives
Allow Black women to vote in state elections

assessment_items.question_content:
**Before the ratification of the Fifteenth Amendment, which of these extended voting rights to African Americans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Reconstruction Acts
The Civil Rights Act of 1866
The Emancipation Proclamation
The Black Codes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which groups were allowed to vote immediately after the ratification of the Fifteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: White men
Black men
White women
Black women

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is true about the voting rights of Black men BEFORE the Civil War?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Five states allowed Black men to vote.
No states allowed Black men to vote.
The right to vote was limited to Black men who owned property.
Only educated Black men could vote.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which political figure supported giving Black men the right to vote?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Frederick Douglass
Andrew Johnson
Henry Clay
Jefferson Davis
